_comment: Radiation Oncology package MR007B for Relapsed Hodgkin's & Non-Hodgkin's Lymphoma
procedure_name: Linear Accelerator, External Beam Radiotherapy IMRT (Intensity Modulated Radiotherapy) (10-25 fractions) (Inclusive of Simulation & Planning Cost) - Adjuvant
min_doctor_qualification: MD / DNB Radiation Oncology or equivalent
clinical_indications: Curative intent for patients with refractory disease after standard chemotherapy
Curative intent for limited relapse not fit for ASCT/ High dose chemotherapy

preauth.label: Clinical notes with planned line of treatment (documents confirming the procedure indicated and details of RT treatment plan)

claim.label: Detailed Discharge Summary (with RT treatment and doses given)

ppd_preauth.q: Is the patient's general condition suitable for radiotherapy?
Is the patient eligible for ASCT?

common_queries: No document to support relapse (no biopsy and no imaging) at pre-authorization
Contraindication (e.g., patient general condition not suitable for radiotherapy) not addressed
Patient's eligibility for ASCT is not clearly stated or justified if applicable.

additional_information.special_empanelment: Essential Medical Personnel: Qualified Medical Oncologist/ Hematologist – for chemotherapy; Radiation Oncologist- for radiation. Essential Medical Infrastructure: Chemotherapy unit/ Ward/ Day care/ Injection room/ LINEAC.
clinical_key_pointers: Relapse usually occurs within first 2 years following initial treatment.
Mostly in patients with advanced disease and less often in early stage disease.
Treatment mostly comprises of salvage therapy (high dose therapy), autologous stem cell transplantation & Involved site Radiation Therapy (ISRT).
Relapse categories for diffuse large B cell lymphoma include: (1) those relapsing after complete remission, (2) partial responders with persistent disease, and (3) refractory patients.
All packages are once in a life-time.
Protocols for low grade NHL (second line and subsequent lines) are listed in NHL template.
additional_information.post_op_care: Follow-up care: OPD visits, blood tests, imaging if required, supportive care medications.
additional_information.referral_criteria: Decide referral of cases to the appropriate level of care (as required) for treatment of patients under PMJAY and selection of corresponding Health Benefit Package.
contraindications: Pts general condition not suitable for radiotherapy
